LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUL-14-2020 HEARING HELD RE: THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT ENTERED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT ENTERED BY THIS COURT ON MARCH 16, 2016 IS GRANTED. THE ADDITIONAL RELIEF SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT RAUL CARL GALAZ IS NOT WARRANTED EITHER BY ANY MOTION REQUESTING SUCH RELIEF OR BY THE RECORD. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR#5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Statement In Response To Mtn To Vacate Judgment Entered
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. SUZANNE C. GRANDT, Esq. of the Office of General Counsel of the the State Bar of California (Tel: 415-538-2388) for plaintiff the State Bar of California;
Defendant RAUL GALAZ (Tel: 210-789-9084) in pro per.

HEARING RE: THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Vacate Judgment Entered.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court indicates it cannot provide defendant legal advice as to the issue the defendant has raised. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The State Bar of California's unopposed motion to vacate the judgment entered by this court on March 16, 2016 is granted. The additional relief sought by Defendant Raul Carl Galaz is not warranted either by any motion requesting such relief or by the record.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)
